revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text:
{{redirect|Template:DL|the equivalent of the HTML {{tag|dl|o}} tag|Template:Glossary|"described" links|Template:Annotated link}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{pp-template|small=yes}}{{High-use}} {{Template shortcut|404|dl|deadlink|dead|Broken link|broken}} This template is used to mark [[WP:Link rot|dead link]]s, either within a paragraph or within a [[WP:Citing sources |reference citation]]. Marking dead links signals to editors and to [[WP:Link rot]] bots that this link needs to be replaced with an archive link. == Usage == Before considering whether to use the {{tl|dead link}} template it is often useful to make a search for an [[Wikipedia:LR#Internet archives|archive copy]] of the dead link and thereby avoid using the tag altogether. All {{cite xxx}} templates have the facility for adding {{para|archive-date}} and {{para|archive-url}} parameters for linking to an archive copy. Non-{{cite xxx}} citations can use {{tl|webarchive}}. Using {{Tl|Dead link}} will place a note by the URL and add the article to one of the [[:Category:Articles with dead external links]] categories. Append this template '''directly after''' the link (after the link code's terminating ] if you are using [[wikitext]]), or, if you are using a {{cite xxx}} template, '''directly after''' the {{cite xxx}} transclusion (not inside it), but inside the reference, if any, i.e. before the , in all cases leaving the original link intact: : {{cite web|url=http://www.example.org|title=The Example Website|access-date=22 September 2012}}{{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} For bare links, leave a space after the end of the URL and the template: :* http://www.example.org {{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Otherwise automated tools can have trouble determining where the URL ends, because it will ambiguously contain [[percent encoding|url-encoding]] and wiki-encoding mixed together. Not following these syntax rules might hinder detection by automated tools. If the citation contains multiple URLs (e.g. chapter-url and url) in a template, and only one is broken, you can use wiki comments like to explain which one is broken. If the article uses [[WP:Footnotes|clickable footnotes]], then this template should be placed just before the </ref> that contains the dead link. The notice will then correctly appear in the reference section instead of in the body of the text, and so [[WP:Link rot]] bots can properly find and fix the link. If you are placing this template inside a reference, do not leave any [[Special:Permalink/1105953472|substitution template]] such as {{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} or {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}, because substitutions fail inside ref tags. Avoid using this template when the reference is fully adequate without the link. In this case, simply remove the link. For example, if a {{tl|cite journal}} template is used with a [[Digital object identifier|DOI]], remove the {{para|url}} parameter. ;Common form (with current date) {{Tlx|Dead link|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} === Optional parameters === ; url: Provides a history of the linked page via the [[Wayback Machine]]. Set '''only''' if history actually exists, as otherwise it is misleading to users and editors. ; date: The date parameter consists of the full English name of the current month with initial capital, a space, and the year, not full dates; e.g., "January 2013", but not "jan13". ''Any deviation from these two rules will result in an "invalid date parameter" error''. ; bot: [[WP:Bots|Bot accounts]] specify the |bot= parameter as a sign that the tag was added by an automated process. Set as bot=. ; fix-attempted: Set this to "yes" if you have tried unsuccessfully to find an archived copy, or a copy with a different URL. This will put the page in [[:Category:Articles with permanently dead external links]], and the annotation will be rendered as "{{dead link |date=July 2022 |fix-attempted=yes}}" instead of just "{{dead link |date=July 2022}}". Note that this currently will occur for any value supplied, including "no", so if a fix was not attempted, simply leave this parameter off, or set it to be blank. Also note that if a page cannot be found in the [[Internet Archive]] (Wayback Machine), [[WP:DEADREF |often a live copy can be found]] via a web search for the full title, in quotes, on the original website, or on the Internet at large. Alternatively, you can use {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} as a shortcut to using this parameter. '''Notes''': * The date-substitution template may be used to automatically generate the date, e.g., {{Dead link|{{subst:DATE}}}}. See [[Help:Substitution]] for more information. * The date= parameter need not be added by the editor: if not entered, a [[Wikipedia:Bots|bot]] will soon add it. == Examples == * {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}}
→ {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}} * {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}}
→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). Frequently software is unable to differentiate the URL from the template, and will interpret the template as a literal part of the URL. Add square brackets or a space separating the template from the URL. Correct: http://www.example.org/ {{Permanent dead link}} and Correct: [http://www.example.org/]{{Permanent dead link}} * Avoid complicated formatting. Nesting inside another template is not generally supported. * Avoid using [ ] { | } < > characters between the end of the external link and {{Permanent dead link}}, as they are often used to format text. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "A template to indicate that the preceding URL is dead", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year the URL was found to be dead, like \"June 2013\"", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true }, "bot": { "label": "Bot name", "description": "The name of the bot used to identify the dead link (not used by regular editors)", "type": "wiki-user-name" }, "url": { "label": "Wayback URL", "description": "The dead URL, used only if there is a Wayback Machine archive", "type": "url" }, "fix-attempted": { "label": "Fix attempted", "description": "Setting to 'yes' (or any non-blank value) indicates that no archive copy is available", "type": "boolean", "example": "yes" } }, "format": "inline" } == See also == * {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} * {{Tl|Full citation needed}} (inline) to be used when a reference suffers from a more severe problem than a mere [[HTTP 404|broken link]] * {{Tl|Citations broken}} * {{Tl|Cleanup bare URLs}} header * {{Tl|Closed access}} and {{Tl|Open access}} citation flags * {{Tl|Dead YouTube link}} * {{Tl|Registration required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Subscription or libraries}} citation flag for when a subscription or library access may be required * {{Tl|Subscription required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Webarchive}} * {{Tl|Website defunct}} to be used in place of the {{tl|URL}} parameter in infoboxes for defunct websites * [[WP:DEADREF|What to do when a reference link goes dead]] * [[Wikipedia:Link rot|Link rot]] === Dead link tools === * Probably of historical interest only: ** [[User:Dispenser/Checklinks|Checklinks]] ** [[User:DeadLinkBOT|DeadLinkBOT]] ** [[User:WebCiteBOT|WebCiteBOT]] {{Inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }}

→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). Frequently software is unable to differentiate the URL from the template, and will interpret the template as a literal part of the URL. Add square brackets or a space separating the template from the URL. Correct: http://www.example.org/ {{Permanent dead link}} and Correct: [http://www.example.org/]{{Permanent dead link}} * Avoid complicated formatting. Nesting inside another template is not generally supported. * Avoid using [ ] { | } < > characters between the end of the external link and {{Permanent dead link}}, as they are often used to format text. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "A template to indicate that the preceding URL is dead", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year the URL was found to be dead, like \"June 2013\"", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true }, "bot": { "label": "Bot name", "description": "The name of the bot used to identify the dead link (not used by regular editors)", "type": "wiki-user-name" }, "url": { "label": "Wayback URL", "description": "The dead URL, used only if there is a Wayback Machine archive", "type": "url" }, "fix-attempted": { "label": "Fix attempted", "description": "Setting to 'yes' (or any non-blank value) indicates that no archive copy is available", "type": "boolean", "example": "yes" } }, "format": "inline" } == See also == * {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} * {{Tl|Full citation needed}} (inline) to be used when a reference suffers from a more severe problem than a mere [[HTTP 404|broken link]] * {{Tl|Citations broken}} * {{Tl|Cleanup bare URLs}} header * {{Tl|Closed access}} and {{Tl|Open access}} citation flags * {{Tl|Dead YouTube link}} * {{Tl|Registration required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Subscription or libraries}} citation flag for when a subscription or library access may be required * {{Tl|Subscription required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Webarchive}} * {{Tl|Website defunct}} to be used in place of the {{tl|URL}} parameter in infoboxes for defunct websites * [[WP:DEADREF|What to do when a reference link goes dead]] * [[Wikipedia:Link rot|Link rot]] === Dead link tools === * Probably of historical interest only: ** [[User:Dispenser/Checklinks|Checklinks]] ** [[User:DeadLinkBOT|DeadLinkBOT]] ** [[User:WebCiteBOT|WebCiteBOT]] {{Inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{redirect|Template:DL|the equivalent of the HTML {{tag|dl|o}} tag|Template:Glossary|"described" links|Template:Annotated link}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{pp-template|small=yes}}{{High-use}} {{Template shortcut|404|dl|deadlink|dead|Broken link|broken}} This template is used to mark [[WP:Link rot|dead link]]s, either within a paragraph or within a [[WP:Citing sources |reference citation]]. Marking dead links signals to editors and to [[WP:Link rot]] bots that this link needs to be replaced with an archive link. == Usage == Before considering whether to use the {{tl|dead link}} template it is often useful to make a search for an [[Wikipedia:LR#Internet archives|archive copy]] of the dead link and thereby avoid using the tag altogether. All {{cite xxx}} templates have the facility for adding {{para|archive-date}} and {{para|archive-url}} parameters for linking to an archive copy. Non-{{cite xxx}} citations can use {{tl|webarchive}}. Using {{Tl|Dead link}} will place a note by the URL and add the article to one of the [[:Category:Articles with dead external links]] categories. Append this template '''directly after''' the link (after the link code's terminating ] if you are using [[wikitext]]), or, if you are using a {{cite xxx}} template, '''directly after''' the {{cite xxx}} transclusion (not inside it), but inside the reference, if any, i.e. before the , in all cases leaving the original link intact: : {{cite web|url=http://www.example.org|title=The Example Website|access-date=22 September 2012}}{{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} For bare links, leave a space after the end of the URL and the template: :* http://www.example.org {{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Otherwise automated tools can have trouble determining where the URL ends, because it will ambiguously contain [[:en:Percent encoding|url-encoding]] and wiki-encoding mixed together. Not following these syntax rules might hinder detection by automated tools. If the citation contains multiple URLs (e.g. chapter-url and url) in a template, and only one is broken, you can use wiki comments like to explain which one is broken. If the article uses [[WP:Footnotes|clickable footnotes]], then this template should be placed just before the </ref> that contains the dead link. The notice will then correctly appear in the reference section instead of in the body of the text, and so [[WP:Link rot]] bots can properly find and fix the link. If you are placing this template inside a reference, do not leave any [[Special:Permalink/1105953472|substitution template]] such as {{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} or {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}, because substitutions fail inside ref tags. Avoid using this template when the reference is fully adequate without the link. In this case, simply remove the link. For example, if a {{tl|cite journal}} template is used with a [[:en:Digital object identifier|DOI]], remove the {{para|url}} parameter. ;Common form (with current date) {{Tlx|Dead link|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} === Optional parameters === ; url: Provides a history of the linked page via the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. Set '''only''' if history actually exists, as otherwise it is misleading to users and editors. ; date: The date parameter consists of the full English name of the current month with initial capital, a space, and the year, not full dates; e.g., "January 2013", but not "jan13". ''Any deviation from these two rules will result in an "invalid date parameter" error''. ; bot: [[WP:Bots|Bot accounts]] specify the |bot= parameter as a sign that the tag was added by an automated process. Set as bot=. ; fix-attempted: Set this to "yes" if you have tried unsuccessfully to find an archived copy, or a copy with a different URL. This will put the page in [[:Category:Articles with permanently dead external links]], and the annotation will be rendered as "{{dead link |date=July 2022 |fix-attempted=yes}}" instead of just "{{dead link |date=July 2022}}". Note that this currently will occur for any value supplied, including "no", so if a fix was not attempted, simply leave this parameter off, or set it to be blank. Also note that if a page cannot be found in the [[:en:Internet Archive|Internet Archive]] (Wayback Machine), [[WP:DEADREF |often a live copy can be found]] via a web search for the full title, in quotes, on the original website, or on the Internet at large. Alternatively, you can use {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} as a shortcut to using this parameter. '''Notes''': * The date-substitution template may be used to automatically generate the date, e.g., {{Dead link|{{subst:DATE}}}}. See [[Help:Substitution]] for more information. * The date= parameter need not be added by the editor: if not entered, a [[Wikipedia:Bots|bot]] will soon add it. == Examples == * {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}}
→ {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}} * {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}}
→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). Frequently software is unable to differentiate the URL from the template, and will interpret the template as a literal part of the URL. Add square brackets or a space separating the template from the URL. Correct: http://www.example.org/ {{Permanent dead link}} and Correct: [http://www.example.org/]{{Permanent dead link}} * Avoid complicated formatting. Nesting inside another template is not generally supported. * Avoid using [ ] { | } < > characters between the end of the external link and {{Permanent dead link}}, as they are often used to format text. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "A template to indicate that the preceding URL is dead", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year the URL was found to be dead, like \"June 2013\"", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true }, "bot": { "label": "Bot name", "description": "The name of the bot used to identify the dead link (not used by regular editors)", "type": "wiki-user-name" }, "url": { "label": "Wayback URL", "description": "The dead URL, used only if there is a Wayback Machine archive", "type": "url" }, "fix-attempted": { "label": "Fix attempted", "description": "Setting to 'yes' (or any non-blank value) indicates that no archive copy is available", "type": "boolean", "example": "yes" } }, "format": "inline" } == See also == * {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} * {{Tl|Full citation needed}} (inline) to be used when a reference suffers from a more severe problem than a mere [[HTTP 404|broken link]] * {{Tl|Citations broken}} * {{Tl|Cleanup bare URLs}} header * {{Tl|Closed access}} and {{Tl|Open access}} citation flags * {{Tl|Dead YouTube link}} * {{Tl|Registration required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Subscription or libraries}} citation flag for when a subscription or library access may be required * {{Tl|Subscription required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Webarchive}} * {{Tl|Website defunct}} to be used in place of the {{tl|URL}} parameter in infoboxes for defunct websites * [[WP:DEADREF|What to do when a reference link goes dead]] * [[Wikipedia:Link rot|Link rot]] === Dead link tools === * Probably of historical interest only: ** [[User:Dispenser/Checklinks|Checklinks]] ** [[User:DeadLinkBOT|DeadLinkBOT]] ** [[User:WebCiteBOT|WebCiteBOT]] {{Inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }}

All {{cite xxx}} templates have the facility for adding {{para|archive-date}} and {{para|archive-url}} parameters for linking to an archive copy. Non-{{cite xxx}} citations can use {{tl|webarchive}}. Using {{Tl|Dead link}} will place a note by the URL and add the article to one of the [[:Category:Articles with dead external links]] categories. Append this template '''directly after''' the link (after the link code's terminating ] if you are using [[:en:Wikitext|Wikitext]]), or, if you are using a {{cite xxx}} template, '''directly after''' the {{cite xxx}} transclusion (not inside it), but inside the reference, if any, i.e. before the , in all cases leaving the original link intact: : {{cite web|url=http://www.example.org|title=The Example Website|access-date=22 September 2012}}{{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} For bare links, leave a space after the end of the URL and the template: :* http://www.example.org {{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Otherwise automated tools can have trouble determining where the URL ends, because it will ambiguously contain [[:en:Percent encoding|url-encoding]] and wiki-encoding mixed together. Not following these syntax rules might hinder detection by automated tools. If the citation contains multiple URLs (e.g. chapter-url and url) in a template, and only one is broken, you can use wiki comments like to explain which one is broken. If the article uses [[WP:Footnotes|clickable footnotes]], then this template should be placed just before the </ref> that contains the dead link. The notice will then correctly appear in the reference section instead of in the body of the text, and so [[WP:Link rot]] bots can properly find and fix the link. If you are placing this template inside a reference, do not leave any [[Special:Permalink/1105953472|substitution template]] such as {{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} or {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}, because substitutions fail inside ref tags. Avoid using this template when the reference is fully adequate without the link. In this case, simply remove the link. For example, if a {{tl|cite journal}} template is used with a [[:en:Digital object identifier|DOI]], remove the {{para|url}} parameter. ;Common form (with current date) {{Tlx|Dead link|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} === Optional parameters === ; url: Provides a history of the linked page via the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. Set '''only''' if history actually exists, as otherwise it is misleading to users and editors. ; date: The date parameter consists of the full English name of the current month with initial capital, a space, and the year, not full dates; e.g., "January 2013", but not "jan13". ''Any deviation from these two rules will result in an "invalid date parameter" error''. ; bot: [[WP:Bots|Bot accounts]] specify the |bot= parameter as a sign that the tag was added by an automated process. Set as bot=. ; fix-attempted: Set this to "yes" if you have tried unsuccessfully to find an archived copy, or a copy with a different URL. This will put the page in [[:Category:Articles with permanently dead external links]], and the annotation will be rendered as "{{dead link |date=July 2022 |fix-attempted=yes}}" instead of just "{{dead link |date=July 2022}}". Note that this currently will occur for any value supplied, including "no", so if a fix was not attempted, simply leave this parameter off, or set it to be blank. Also note that if a page cannot be found in the [[:en:Internet Archive|Internet Archive]] (Wayback Machine), [[WP:DEADREF |often a live copy can be found]] via a web search for the full title, in quotes, on the original website, or on the Internet at large. Alternatively, you can use {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} as a shortcut to using this parameter. '''Notes''': * The date-substitution template may be used to automatically generate the date, e.g., {{Dead link|{{subst:DATE}}}}. See [[Help:Substitution]] for more information. * The date= parameter need not be added by the editor: if not entered, a [[Wikipedia:Bots|bot]] will soon add it. == Examples == * {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}}
→ {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}} * {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}}
→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). Frequently software is unable to differentiate the URL from the template, and will interpret the template as a literal part of the URL. Add square brackets or a space separating the template from the URL. Correct: http://www.example.org/ {{Permanent dead link}} and Correct: [http://www.example.org/]{{Permanent dead link}} * Avoid complicated formatting. Nesting inside another template is not generally supported. * Avoid using [ ] { | } < > characters between the end of the external link and {{Permanent dead link}}, as they are often used to format text. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "A template to indicate that the preceding URL is dead", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year the URL was found to be dead, like \"June 2013\"", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true }, "bot": { "label": "Bot name", "description": "The name of the bot used to identify the dead link (not used by regular editors)", "type": "wiki-user-name" }, "url": { "label": "Wayback URL", "description": "The dead URL, used only if there is a Wayback Machine archive", "type": "url" }, "fix-attempted": { "label": "Fix attempted", "description": "Setting to 'yes' (or any non-blank value) indicates that no archive copy is available", "type": "boolean", "example": "yes" } }, "format": "inline" } == See also == * {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} * {{Tl|Full citation needed}} (inline) to be used when a reference suffers from a more severe problem than a mere [[HTTP 404|broken link]] * {{Tl|Citations broken}} * {{Tl|Cleanup bare URLs}} header * {{Tl|Closed access}} and {{Tl|Open access}} citation flags * {{Tl|Dead YouTube link}} * {{Tl|Registration required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Subscription or libraries}} citation flag for when a subscription or library access may be required * {{Tl|Subscription required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Webarchive}} * {{Tl|Website defunct}} to be used in place of the {{tl|URL}} parameter in infoboxes for defunct websites * [[WP:DEADREF|What to do when a reference link goes dead]] * [[Wikipedia:Link rot|Link rot]] === Dead link tools === * Probably of historical interest only: ** [[User:Dispenser/Checklinks|Checklinks]] ** [[User:DeadLinkBOT|DeadLinkBOT]] ** [[User:WebCiteBOT|WebCiteBOT]] {{Inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

{{redirect|Template:DL|the equivalent of the HTML {{tag|dl|o}} tag|Template:Glossary|"described" links|Template:Annotated link}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{pp-template|small=yes}}{{High-use}} {{Template shortcut|404|dl|deadlink|dead|Broken link|broken}} This template is used to mark [[WP:Link rot|dead link]]s, either within a paragraph or within a [[WP:Citing sources |reference citation]]. Marking dead links signals to editors and to [[WP:Link rot]] bots that this link needs to be replaced with an archive link. == Usage == Before considering whether to use the {{tl|dead link}} template it is often useful to make a search for an [[Wikipedia:LR#Internet archives|archive copy]] of the dead link and thereby avoid using the tag altogether. All {{cite xxx}} templates have the facility for adding {{para|archive-date}} and {{para|archive-url}} parameters for linking to an archive copy. Non-{{cite xxx}} citations can use {{tl|webarchive}}. Using {{Tl|Dead link}} will place a note by the URL and add the article to one of the [[:Category:Articles with dead external links]] categories. Not following these syntax rules might hinder detection by automated tools. If the citation contains multiple URLs (e.g. chapter-url and url) in a template, and only one is broken, you can use wiki comments like to explain which one is broken. If the article uses [[WP:Footnotes|clickable footnotes]], then this template should be placed just before the </ref> that contains the dead link. The notice will then correctly appear in the reference section instead of in the body of the text, and so [[WP:Link rot]] bots can properly find and fix the link. If you are placing this template inside a reference, do not leave any [[Special:Permalink/1105953472|substitution template]] such as {{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} or {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}, because substitutions fail inside ref tags. Avoid using this template when the reference is fully adequate without the link. In this case, simply remove the link. For example, if a {{tl|cite journal}} template is used with a [[:en:Digital object identifier|DOI]], remove the {{para|url}} parameter. ;Common form (with current date) {{Tlx|Dead link|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} === Optional parameters === ; url: Provides a history of the linked page via the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. Set '''only''' if history actually exists, as otherwise it is misleading to users and editors. ; date: The date parameter consists of the full English name of the current month with initial capital, a space, and the year, not full dates; e.g., "January 2013", but not "jan13". ''Any deviation from these two rules will result in an "invalid date parameter" error''. ; bot: [[WP:Bots|Bot accounts]] specify the |bot= parameter as a sign that the tag was added by an automated process. Set as bot=. ; fix-attempted: Set this to "yes" if you have tried unsuccessfully to find an archived copy, or a copy with a different URL. This will put the page in [[:Category:Articles with permanently dead external links]], and the annotation will be rendered as "{{dead link |date=July 2022 |fix-attempted=yes}}" instead of just "{{dead link |date=July 2022}}". Note that this currently will occur for any value supplied, including "no", so if a fix was not attempted, simply leave this parameter off, or set it to be blank. Also note that if a page cannot be found in the [[:en:Internet Archive|Internet Archive]] (Wayback Machine), [[WP:DEADREF |often a live copy can be found]] via a web search for the full title, in quotes, on the original website, or on the Internet at large. Alternatively, you can use {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} as a shortcut to using this parameter. '''Notes''': * The date-substitution template may be used to automatically generate the date, e.g., {{Dead link|{{subst:DATE}}}}. See [[Help:Substitution]] for more information. * The date= parameter need not be added by the editor: if not entered, a [[Wikipedia:Bots|bot]] will soon add it. == Examples == * {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}}
→ {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}} * {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}}
→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). See [[Help:Substitution]] for more information. * The date= parameter need not be added by the editor: if not entered, a [[Wikipedia:Bots|bot]] will soon add it. == Examples == * {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}}
→ {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}} * {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}}
→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). Frequently software is unable to differentiate the URL from the template, and will interpret the template as a literal part of the URL. Add square brackets or a space separating the template from the URL. Correct: http://www.example.org/ {{Permanent dead link}} and Correct: [http://www.example.org/]{{Permanent dead link}} * Avoid complicated formatting. Nesting inside another template is not generally supported. * Avoid using [ ] { | } < > characters between the end of the external link and {{Permanent dead link}}, as they are often used to format text. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "A template to indicate that the preceding URL is dead", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year the URL was found to be dead, like \"June 2013\"", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true }, "bot": { "label": "Bot name", "description": "The name of the bot used to identify the dead link (not used by regular editors)", "type": "wiki-user-name" }, "url": { "label": "Wayback URL", "description": "The dead URL, used only if there is a Wayback Machine archive", "type": "url" }, "fix-attempted": { "label": "Fix attempted", "description": "Setting to 'yes' (or any non-blank value) indicates that no archive copy is available", "type": "boolean", "example": "yes" } }, "format": "inline" } == See also == * {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} * {{Tl|Full citation needed}} (inline) to be used when a reference suffers from a more severe problem than a mere [[HTTP 404|broken link]] * {{Tl|Citations broken}} * {{Tl|Cleanup bare URLs}} header * {{Tl|Closed access}} and {{Tl|Open access}} citation flags * {{Tl|Dead YouTube link}} * {{Tl|Registration required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Subscription or libraries}} citation flag for when a subscription or library access may be required * {{Tl|Subscription required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Webarchive}} * {{Tl|Website defunct}} to be used in place of the {{tl|URL}} parameter in infoboxes for defunct websites * [[WP:DEADREF|What to do when a reference link goes dead]] * [[Wikipedia:Link rot|Link rot]] === Dead link tools === * Probably of historical interest only: ** [[User:Dispenser/Checklinks|Checklinks]] ** [[User:DeadLinkBOT|DeadLinkBOT]] ** [[User:WebCiteBOT|WebCiteBOT]] {{Inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Dead_link%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{redirect|Template:DL|the equivalent of the HTML {{tag|dl|o}} tag|Template:Glossary|"described" links|Template:Annotated link}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{pp-template|small=yes}}{{High-use}} {{Template shortcut|404|dl|deadlink|dead|Broken link|broken}} This template is used to mark [[WP:Link rot|dead link]]s, either within a paragraph or within a [[WP:Citing sources |reference citation]]. Marking dead links signals to editors and to [[WP:Link rot]] bots that this link needs to be replaced with an archive link. == Usage == Before considering whether to use the {{tl|dead link}} template it is often useful to make a search for an [[Wikipedia:LR#Internet archives|archive copy]] of the dead link and thereby avoid using the tag altogether. All {{cite xxx}} templates have the facility for adding {{para|archive-date}} and {{para|archive-url}} parameters for linking to an archive copy. Non-{{cite xxx}} citations can use {{tl|webarchive}}. Using {{Tl|Dead link}} will place a note by the URL and add the article to one of the [[:Category:Articles with dead external links]] categories. Append this template '''directly after''' the link (after the link code's terminating ] if you are using [[:en:Wikitext|Wikitext]]), or, if you are using a {{cite xxx}} template, '''directly after''' the {{cite xxx}} transclusion (not inside it), but inside the reference, if any, i.e. before the , in all cases leaving the original link intact: : {{cite web|url=http://www.example.org|title=The Example Website|access-date=22 September 2012}}{{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} For bare links, leave a space after the end of the URL and the template: :* http://www.example.org {{Dead link|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} Otherwise automated tools can have trouble determining where the URL ends, because it will ambiguously contain [[:en:Percent encoding|url-encoding]] and wiki-encoding mixed together. Not following these syntax rules might hinder detection by automated tools. If the citation contains multiple URLs (e.g. chapter-url and url) in a template, and only one is broken, you can use wiki comments like to explain which one is broken. If the article uses [[WP:Footnotes|clickable footnotes]], then this template should be placed just before the </ref> that contains the dead link. The notice will then correctly appear in the reference section instead of in the body of the text, and so [[WP:Link rot]] bots can properly find and fix the link. If you are placing this template inside a reference, do not leave any [[Special:Permalink/1105953472|substitution template]] such as {{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} or {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}, because substitutions fail inside ref tags. Avoid using this template when the reference is fully adequate without the link. In this case, simply remove the link. For example, if a {{tl|cite journal}} template is used with a [[:en:Digital object identifier|DOI]], remove the {{para|url}} parameter. ;Common form (with current date) {{Tlx|Dead link|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} === Optional parameters === ; url: Provides a history of the linked page via the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. Set '''only''' if history actually exists, as otherwise it is misleading to users and editors. ; date: The date parameter consists of the full English name of the current month with initial capital, a space, and the year, not full dates; e.g., "January 2013", but not "jan13". ''Any deviation from these two rules will result in an "invalid date parameter" error''. ; bot: [[WP:Bots|Bot accounts]] specify the |bot= parameter as a sign that the tag was added by an automated process. Set as bot=. ; fix-attempted: Set this to "yes" if you have tried unsuccessfully to find an archived copy, or a copy with a different URL. This will put the page in [[:Category:Articles with permanently dead external links]], and the annotation will be rendered as "{{dead link |date=July 2022 |fix-attempted=yes}}" instead of just "{{dead link |date=July 2022}}". Note that this currently will occur for any value supplied, including "no", so if a fix was not attempted, simply leave this parameter off, or set it to be blank. Also note that if a page cannot be found in the [[:en:Internet Archive|Internet Archive]] (Wayback Machine), [[WP:DEADREF |often a live copy can be found]] via a web search for the full title, in quotes, on the original website, or on the Internet at large. Alternatively, you can use {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} as a shortcut to using this parameter. '''Notes''': * The date-substitution template may be used to automatically generate the date, e.g., {{Dead link|{{subst:DATE}}}}. See [[Help:Substitution]] for more information. * The date= parameter need not be added by the editor: if not entered, a [[Wikipedia:Bots|bot]] will soon add it. == Examples == * {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}}
→ {{cite web |url=http://www.example.org |title=Web page title |website=Example.com |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link |date=September 2012}} * {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}}
→ {{cite news |title=Sorrell accuses Murdoch of panic buying |author=Plunkett, John |url=http://media.guardian.co.uk/site/story/0,14173,1601858,00.html |newspaper=[[:en:The Guardian|The Guardian]] |date=27 October 2005 |access-date=22 September 2012}} {{Dead link}} {{Reflist}} === Caveats === * Avoid using free/bare links without a separator (e.g. http://www.example.org/{{Permanent dead link}}). Frequently software is unable to differentiate the URL from the template, and will interpret the template as a literal part of the URL. Add square brackets or a space separating the template from the URL. Correct: http://www.example.org/ {{Permanent dead link}} and Correct: [http://www.example.org/]{{Permanent dead link}} * Avoid complicated formatting. Nesting inside another template is not generally supported. * Avoid using [ ] { | } < > characters between the end of the external link and {{Permanent dead link}}, as they are often used to format text. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "A template to indicate that the preceding URL is dead", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year the URL was found to be dead, like \"June 2013\"", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true }, "bot": { "label": "Bot name", "description": "The name of the bot used to identify the dead link (not used by regular editors)", "type": "wiki-user-name" }, "url": { "label": "Wayback URL", "description": "The dead URL, used only if there is a Wayback Machine archive", "type": "url" }, "fix-attempted": { "label": "Fix attempted", "description": "Setting to 'yes' (or any non-blank value) indicates that no archive copy is available", "type": "boolean", "example": "yes" } }, "format": "inline" } == See also == * {{Tl|Permanent dead link}} * {{Tl|Full citation needed}} (inline) to be used when a reference suffers from a more severe problem than a mere [[HTTP 404|broken link]] * {{Tl|Citations broken}} * {{Tl|Cleanup bare URLs}} header * {{Tl|Closed access}} and {{Tl|Open access}} citation flags * {{Tl|Dead YouTube link}} * {{Tl|Registration required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Subscription or libraries}} citation flag for when a subscription or library access may be required * {{Tl|Subscription required}} citation flag * {{Tl|Webarchive}} * {{Tl|Website defunct}} to be used in place of the {{tl|URL}} parameter in infoboxes for defunct websites * [[WP:DEADREF|What to do when a reference link goes dead]] * [[Wikipedia:Link rot|Link rot]] === Dead link tools === * Probably of historical interest only: ** [[User:Dispenser/Checklinks|Checklinks]] ** [[User:DeadLinkBOT|DeadLinkBOT]] ** [[User:WebCiteBOT|WebCiteBOT]] {{Inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:External link maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
